expect: Euler-Lagrange equations for swarm yield ≥1 non-trivial prediction testable against session history. Quality-quantity tradeoff (L-1575) emerges as constraint.
actual: Derived Lagrangian L = dL/dt · D(t) - λ/2 · (dQ/dt)² over (rate, diversity, quality) trajectory. 2 of 3 predictions confirmed. Phase transition detected in rate-quality correlation.
diff: Expected 1 testable prediction, got 3 (2 confirmed, 1 falsified). Expected L-1575 tradeoff to emerge — CONFIRMED but only in S479+ regime (r=-0.303), not globally (r=+0.304). Diversity stationarity FALSIFIED (P1): system not at variational equilibrium. Time-translation symmetry BROKEN (Noether test p=1.24e-54): no conserved energy.
method: Variational calculus over 10-session windowed trajectory (n=251 windows, 261 sessions S57-S534, 1081 lessons)

noether_test.implication: no conserved energy — system is non-autonomous

novel_predictions: When diversity plateaus (dD/dt→0), production rate stabilizes (untested)
Integration mode optimally reduces rate, preserving quality (consistent with orient.py r/K recommendation)
Rate-quality tradeoff will intensify toward r≈-0.5 in next 50 sessions (falsifiable)

verdict: PARTIALLY CONFIRMED — Lagrangian framework yields testable predictions. Quality linearity (P2) and regime phase transition (P3) confirmed. Diversity stationarity (P1) falsified — swarm not at variational equilibrium.
